params.name: offset
params.description: Pagination cursor from a previous response to fetch the next page.

items.docs: Returns a paginated list of all bases that the authenticated user has access to. Each base includes its ID, name, and permission level. Use the offset parameter from the response to paginate through all available bases.
Creates a new base in the specified workspace. The request must include the workspace ID, base name, and at least one table definition with fields. Returns the newly created base with its schema.

params.name: baseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

params.name: include
params.description: Additional information to include in the response. Pass visibleFieldIds to include visible field IDs for each view.

items.docs: Returns the schema of the specified base, including all tables, their fields (with type and configuration), and views. This endpoint provides a complete structural overview of the base.

info.name: Airtable Get Current User and Scopes

items.docs: Returns the ID and scopes of the currently authenticated user or service account. Useful for verifying authentication and checking available permissions.

info.name: Airtable Create a Table in a Base

params.name: baseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

items.docs: Creates a new table within the specified base. The table must include a name and at least one field definition. Optionally include a description for the table.

params.name: baseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

params.name: tableId
params.description: The unique identifier of the table (starts with 'tbl').

items.docs: Updates the name and/or description of an existing table. Only the provided fields will be modified.

info.name: Airtable Create a Field in a Table

params.name: baseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

params.name: tableId
params.description: The unique identifier of the table (starts with 'tbl').

items.docs: Creates a new field in the specified table. The field must include a name and type. Some field types (like singleSelect, multipleSelects) accept additional options for configuration. Note that formula and some computed field types cannot be created via the API.

params.name: baseId
params.description: The unique identifier of the base (starts with 'app').

params.name: tableId
params.description: The unique identifier of the table (starts with 'tbl').

params.name: fieldId
params.description: The unique identifier of the field (starts with 'fld').

items.docs: Updates the name, description, or configuration options of an existing field. Only the provided properties will be modified. Not all field properties can be updated after creation.
